questions.question_html: For the differential equation {tex}xy\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left( {x + 2\;} \right)(\;y + 2){/tex} find the solution curve passing through the point (1, –1).
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{ydy}{y+2}=\frac{(x+2)dx}{x} {/tex} {tex}\int \frac{ydy}{y+2}=\int \frac{(x+2)dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{y+2-2dy}{y+2}=\int \frac{(x+2)dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\int dy-\int \frac{2}{y+2}=\int dx+\int \frac{2}{x}{/tex} {tex}y-2log|y+2|=x+2log|x|+c{/tex} Here x=1 and y=-1 implies {tex}-1-2log|-1+2|=1+2log|1|+c\implies-1-2log|1|=1+c \because log|1|=0\implies\therefore c=-2{/tex} Hence, {tex}y-2log|y+2|=x+2log|x|-2{/tex} {tex}y-x+2=2log|x|+2log|y+2|{/tex} {tex}y-x+2=2log|x(y+2)|{/tex} {tex}\\y-x+2=log|x^{2}(y+2)^{2}|{/tex}

questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=e^{3x}e^{4y}{/tex} {tex}\int {e^{-4y}}dy=\int e^{3x}dx{/tex} {tex}\frac{-e^{-4y}}{4}=\frac{e^{3x}}{3}+c{/tex} here x = y =0 gives {tex}\\\frac{-1}{4}=\frac{1}{3}+c{/tex} {tex}\therefore c=\frac{-7}{12}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{-e^{-4y}}{4}=\frac{e^{3x}}{3}-\frac{7}{12}{/tex} {tex}4e^{3x}+3e^{-4y}-7=0{/tex}

questions.question_text: In a bank, the principal increases continuously at the rate of r% per year. Find the value of r if Rs.100 double itself in 10 years (log e 2 = 0.6931).
questions.question_html: In a bank, the principal increases continuously at the rate of r% per year. Find the value of r if Rs.100 double itself in 10 years (loge2 = 0.6931).
questions.explanation: Let P be the principal at any time t. then, {tex}\frac{{dP}}{{dt}} = \frac{{rP}}{{100}} \Rightarrow \frac{{dP}}{{dt}} = \frac{P}{{100}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \int {\frac{1}{P}dP = \int {\frac{r}{{100}}dt} } {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log P = \frac{r}{{100}}t + \log c{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log \frac{P}{c} = \frac{r}{{100}}t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow P = c{e^{\frac{r}{{100}}}}{/tex} When P = 100 and t = 0., then, c = 100, Thus, we have: {tex}\Rightarrow P=100 \ {{e}^{{}^{r}\!\!\diagup\!\!{}_{100}\;}}{/tex} Now, suppose t = T, when P = 100., then; {tex}\Rightarrow 200 = 100{e^{\frac{T}{{100}}}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {e^{\frac{T}{{100}}}} = 2{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow T = 100\log 2{/tex} = 100(0.6931) = 6.93%. Which is the required solution

questions.explanation: Here P is the principal at time t {tex}\frac{{dP}}{{dt}} = \frac{{5P}}{{100}} \Rightarrow \frac{{dP}}{{dt}} = \frac{P}{{20}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \int_{}^{} {\frac{1}{P}dP = \int_{}^{} {\frac{1}{{20}}dt} } {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log P = \frac{1}{{20}}t + \log c{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log \frac{P}{c} = \frac{1}{{20}}t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow P =c {e^{\frac{1}{{100}}}}{/tex} When P = 1000 and t = 0 ., then , c = 1000, therefore, we have : {tex} \Rightarrow P = 1000{e^{\frac{T}{{100}}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow A = 1000{e^{\frac{5}{{10}}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {e^{\frac{5}{{10}}}} = A{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow A = 1000\log 0.5{/tex} = 1000(1.648) = 1648

questions.question_text: Find the particular solution for (x + y)dy + (x –y) dx =0; y=1 when x =1
questions.question_html: Find the particular solution for (x + y)dy + (x –y) dx =0; y=1 when x =1
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{y-x}{x+y}{/tex} it is a homogenous equation Hence we put y=vx {tex}x = {-b \pm \sqrt{b^2-4ac} \over 2a}let \ y=vx{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{\mathrm{dv} }{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{\mathrm{dv} }{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{vx-x}{vx+x}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-1}{v+1}-v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-1-v^{2}-v}{v+1}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{(v+1) dv}{1+v^{2}}=-\int \frac{dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{vdv}{1+v^{2}}+\int \frac{dv}{1+v^{2}}=-\int \frac{dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}log|1+v^{2}|+tan^{-1}v=-logx+c{/tex} {tex}log|1+v^{2}|+2tan^{-1}v+2logx=c {/tex} Resubstituting v=y/x we get {tex}log|\frac{x^2+y^2}{x^2}|+2tan^{-1}\frac{y}{x}+2logx=c{/tex} When x=y=1 we get, {tex}log|\frac{1^2+1^2}{1^2}|+2tan^{-1}\frac{1}{1}+log1=c{/tex} {tex}\\c=log2+\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}log|\frac{x^2+y^2}{x^2}|+2tan^{-1}\frac{y}{x}+2logx=log2+\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\\log|{x^2+y^2}|+2tan^{-1}\frac{y}{x}=log2+\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

questions.question_html: General solution of {tex}x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y - x + xycot\;x = 0\;(x\; \ne 0){/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + (\frac{1}{x} + \cot x)y = 1 \Rightarrow P = \left( {\frac{1}{x} + \cot x} \right),Q = 1{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I.F. = {e^{\int {\left( {\frac{1}{x} + \cot x} \right)} dx}} = {e^{\log x + \log \sin x}} = {e^{\log (x\sin x)}} = xsinx{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y(x\sin x) = \int_{}^{} {1.x\sin x \Rightarrow } xy\sin x = - x\cos x + \sin x + c{/tex} {tex}xysinx=-xcosx+sinx+c {/tex} Dividing by xsinx, we get {tex} y=-cotx+\frac{1}{x}+\frac{c}{xsinx}{/tex} It is a linear differential equation in y in the form of {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+Py=Q{/tex} hence solution is {tex}y.IF=\int IF\ \ Q(x)dx+c{/tex}

questions.explanation: {tex}({x^2} - {y^2})dx + 2xydy = 0 \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{y^3} - {x^3}}}{{2xy}}{/tex} Put y = vx, we have; {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = v + x\frac{{dv}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{y^2} - {x^2}}}{{2xy}} \Rightarrow v + x\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{v^2}{x^2} - {x^2}}}{{2xvx}} \Rightarrow x\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = - \left( {\frac{{{v^2} + 1}}{{2v}}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \int_{}^{} {\frac{{2v}}{{{v^2} + 1}}dv} = - \int_{}^{} {\frac{1}{x}dx \Rightarrow \log |{v^2} + 1| = - \log |x| + \log C} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log |{v^2} + 1| + \log |x| = \log C \Rightarrow ({v^2} + 1)|x| = C \Rightarrow ({x^2} + {y^2}) = Cx{/tex}

questions.explanation: {tex}{{x}^{2}}dy+(xy+{{y}^{2}})dx=0\Rightarrow \frac{dy}{dx}=-\left( \frac{xy+{{y}^{2}}}{{{x}^{2}}} \right){/tex} Put {tex}y = vx \ and \ \frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow v + x\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = - (v + {v^2}){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x\frac{dv}{dx}=-2v-v^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow xdv=-(v^2+2v)dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int{\frac{1}{v^2+2v}}dv=-\int{\frac{1}{x}}dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int{\frac{1}{(v+1)^2-1^2}}dv=-\int{\frac{1}{x}}dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{v}{{v + 2}}} \right| = - \log x + \log c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{v}{{v + 2}}} \right| +logx^2= - \log k {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{vx^2}{{v + 2}}} \right| = \log k {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left| {\frac{{v{x^2}}}{{v + 2}}} \right| = k \Rightarrow \frac{{{x^2}y}}{{y + 2x}} = k{/tex} When x = 1, and y = 1, k = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{x}^{2}}y}{y+2x}=\frac{1}{3}\Rightarrow 3{{x}^{2}}y=y+2x{/tex}

questions.question_html: Solution of (x – y) dy – (x + y) dx = 0 is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex} Let y = vx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}\\v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{1+v}{1-v}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{1+v}{1-v}-v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{1+v-v+v^2}{1-v}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1-v}{1+v^2}dv=\frac{dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{dv}{1-v}-\int \frac{vdv}{1+v^2}=\int \frac{dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}tan^{-1}v-\frac{1}{2}log|1+v^2|=logx+c{/tex} {tex}2tan^{-1}\frac{y}{x}=log|1+\frac{y^2}{x^2}|+logx^2+c{/tex} {tex}ta{n^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{y}{x}} \right) = \;\frac{1}{2}\log \left( {{x^2} + \;{y^2}} \right) + \;C{/tex}

questions.question_html: Solution of differential equation {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{x + y}}{{x - y}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{x + y}}{{x - y}}{/tex} Put y = vx and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = v + x\frac{{dv}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{x + y}}{{x - y}} \Rightarrow v + x\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = \frac{{1 + v}}{{1 - v}} \Rightarrow x\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = \frac{{1 + {v^2}}}{{1 - v}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \int_{}^{} {\frac{{1 - v}}{{1 + {v^2}}}dv = \int_{}^{} {\frac{1}{x}dx} \Rightarrow \int_{}^{} {\frac{1}{{1 + {v^2}}}dv - \frac{1}{2}\int_{}^{} {\frac{{2v}}{{1 + {v^2}}}dv = \int_{}^{} {\frac{1}{x}dx} } } } {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}v - \frac{1}{2}\log (1 + {v^2}) = \log x + C{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{y}{x}} \right) = \frac{1}{2}\log ({x^2} + {y^2}) + C{/tex}

questions.question_html: General solution of {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + \;2y = \sin x{/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex} \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 2y = \sin x \Rightarrow P = 2,Q = \sin x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I.F. = {e^{\int {2dx} }} = {e^{2x}} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y{e^{2x}} = \int {\sin x.{e^{2x}}dx \Rightarrow } y{e^{2x}} {/tex} {tex}= {e^{2x}}\frac{1}{5}\left( {2\sin x - \cos x} \right) + C{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y = \frac{1}{5}\left( {2\sin x - \cos x} \right) + C{e^{ - 2x}} {/tex}

questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 3y = {e^{ - 2x}} \Rightarrow P = 3,Q = {e^{ - 2x}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I.F. = {e^{\int_{}^{} {3.dx} }} = {e^{3x}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y.{e^{3x}} = \int_{}^{} {{e^{ - 2x}}{e^{3x}}dx} \Rightarrow y.{e^{3x}} = \int_{}^{} {{e^x}dx \Rightarrow y.{e^{3x}} = {e^x} + C} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y = {e^{ - 2x}} + C{e^{ - 3x}}{/tex}

questions.question_html: General solution of {tex}x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 2y = {x^2}\log x{/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+\frac{2}{x}y=xlogx{/tex} {tex}ye^{\int \frac{2dx}{x}}=\int e^{\int \frac{2dx}{x}}xlogx+C{/tex} {tex}yx^2=\int x^3logxdx+c{/tex} {tex}yx^2=logx \int x^3dx-\int \int x^3dx \frac{d}{dx}logx+c {/tex} {tex}yx^2=logx \frac{x^4}{4}-\int \frac{x^3}{4}dx+c{/tex} {tex}yx^2= \frac{x^4}{4}logx-\frac{x^4}{16}+c{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{x^2}{4}logx-\frac{x^2}{16}+cx^{-2}{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{x^2}{16}(4logx-1)+cx^{-2}\\{/tex}

questions.question_html: General solution of {tex}xlogx\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y = \frac{2}{x}logx{/tex}  is
questions.explanation: {tex}xlogx\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y = \frac{2}{x}logx\;{/tex} Dividing by xlogx we get {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + \frac{1}{{xlogx}}y = \frac{2}{{{x^2}}}{/tex} it is a linear diff equation we get, {tex}y{e^{\int {\frac{{dx}}{{xlogx}}} }} = \int {\frac{2}{{{x^2}}}} {e^{\int {\frac{{dx}}{{xlogx}}} }}dx + c{/tex} {tex}ylogx = \int {\frac{2}{{{x^2}}}} logxdx + c{/tex} {tex}ylogx = 2[\frac{{ - logx}}{x} - \frac{1}{x}] + c{/tex} {tex}ylogx = - \frac{2}{x}(1 + logx) + c{/tex} Since {tex}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} logxdx = logx\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} dx - \int {\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} } dx\frac{{\text{d}}}{{{\text{d}}x}}logxdx{/tex} {tex}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} logxdx = \frac{{ - 1}}{x}logx - \int {\frac{{ - 1}}{x}} \frac{1}{x}dx + c{/tex} {tex}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} logxdx = \frac{{ - 1}}{x}logx - \frac{1}{x} + c{/tex}

questions.question_html: General solution of {tex}\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)dy + 2xy\;dx = \cot x\;dx\;(x \ne 0){/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}(1+x^2)dy=(cotx-2xy)dx{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{cotx-2xy}{1+x^2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+\frac{2x}{1+x^2}y=\frac{cotx}{1+x^2}{/tex} It is a linear differential equation in y. Therefore, Solution is {tex}\\ye^{\int \frac{2xdx}{1+x^2}}=\int \frac{cotx}{1+x^2}e^{\int \frac{2xdx}{1+x^2}}dx+c{/tex} {tex}y(1+x^2)=\int \frac{cotx}{1+x^2}(1+x^2)dx+c{/tex} {tex}y(1+x^2)=\int cotxdx+c{/tex} {tex}y(1+x^2)=log|sinx|+c{/tex}
